extra.reason: Implemented: bidirectional leg-list↔map hover highlight (emphasizeLeg + legLayers) and sticky 'insert waypoint' tooltip + copy cursor on route polylines.
Implemented: bidirectional leg-list↔map hover highlight (emphasizeLeg + legLayers) and sticky 'insert waypoint' tooltip + copy cursor on route polylines.
Added .github/workflows/pages.yml: assembles index.html+src into _site and deploys via GitHub Pages Actions on push to master. Needs one-time repo setting: Pages → Source → GitHub Actions.

Direct legs now sample the swisstopo swissAlti3D DEM (profile.json, sr=2056) for ascent, via a sub-metre WGS84->LV95 approximate formula (no proj4). Elevation fetched after the path draws, folded into the ascent stat; best-effort, cached, failure-tolerant. Tests + fixture added.

Reframed from single-call batching to concurrent per-leg fetches. True straight= batching returns one merged track and would regress the per-leg model (hover highlight, Routed/Direct toggle, dashed-direct styling, error fallback, DEM elevation). Instead recompute now fires all routed-leg BRouter requests concurrently (were sequential), preserving one polyline per leg and all per-leg features. Big wall-clock win on multi-leg routes; same request count.

Map now renders natively in LV95/EPSG:2056 via proj4 + proj4leaflet. Tile grid (resolutions, origin [2420000,1350000], {z}/{x}/{y} ordering) verified against a live central-Zürich tile; end-to-end verified via live smoke test + a headless screenshot showing correct alignment, routing, and elevation. OpenTopoMap fallback dropped (Web-Mercator can't align in LV95); tile-block path is now warning-only. Tests + README updated.

Implemented: 'swisstopo overlays' panel group with a checkbox list (curated: Veloland cycling, mountain-bike, public-transport stops). Each toggles an L.tileLayer (transparent PNG, same LV95 grid, zIndex above base but below route/markers). Layer set is a one-line data array. Verified via test (overlay tile URL shape) + headless screenshot showing overlays render over the base.

Implemented: right-click / long-press (contextmenu) opens an L.popup identifying features at that point via swisstopo's identify REST API (LV95-native). Queries enabled overlays + swissnames3d; shows a deduped, HTML-escaped list (layer + feature name); friendly empty/error states; left-click still only adds waypoints. Verified via stubbed test + a live-data screenshot (transit stop + place names). Panel hint added.

Implemented: 'Send to phone' button uses the Web Share API to hand the GPX file to the OS share sheet (→ Garmin Connect / any app), no download/upload. buildGpx() extracted and shared by export+share; button revealed only where navigator.canShare({files}) is true (mobile), else Export GPX download remains. Verified via test with mocked Web Share (button shows, shares topo-route.gpx). Direct Connect upload confirmed infeasible client-side; this is the backend-free path.

Each leg now has a transparent 28px-wide hit polyline over the thin visible line, carrying the click/hover/tooltip; visible line is interactive:false. Tapping within ~14px of a leg inserts a waypoint instead of appending. Verified by a test clicking ~7px off a straight direct leg (mid Via inserted, Finish unchanged); existing path-count assertions scoped to visible lines.
Implemented in pages.yml assemble step (sed ?v=<sha8> onto app.js/styles.css).

Won't-fix (iOS platform limitation). Confirmed on-device: even a clean single-file application/gpx+xml Web Share does not surface Garmin Connect in the iOS share sheet — Garmin's share extension won't accept a browser-synthesized file the way it accepts a native app's file share. Not fixable from a web app. Workaround: the share sheet's 'Save to Files' saves topo-route.gpx, then import into Garmin. Net wins retained: removed the buggy title/text multi-item share, standard gpx+xml MIME, and deploy cache-busting. 'Send to phone' still works for other apps and Android.
